dimensions.rationale: Immutable diff is limited to Stage 6 assurance code, contracts, synthetic evidence, tests, lock/SBOM, and supporting CI; the run contract explicitly excludes Stage 7 and protected execution.

The capacity gate omits PRODUCTION_RUN from capacity-controlled operations, so UNKNOWN/RED capacity authorizes its supplied callable. The capacity test omits that operation, leaving the claimed gate coverage unsupported.

KILL-007 retains production_enabled and the operational gate permits PRODUCTION_RUN despite RED/UNKNOWN capacity. Therefore the asserted complete Kill Criterion/capacity protection does not hold for every exposed sensitive callable.
The change declares a single local-commit revert boundary, local-only delivery, zero protected-oracle execution, and no claimed remote state change.

findings.finding: OperationalGate.execute permits SensitiveOperation.PRODUCTION_RUN when capacity is UNKNOWN or RED because PRODUCTION_RUN is absent from _CAPACITY_WRITES; KILL-007 also leaves production_enabled true. The capacity and Kill evidence therefore does not prove callable side effects are fail-closed under capacity failure.
findings.required_fix: Make PRODUCTION_RUN fail closed for UNKNOWN/RED capacity and KILL-007, or prove it cannot cause side effects and remove it from the sensitive-operation surface; add direct UNKNOWN/RED and KILL-007 regression tests, then repeat independent review.

limitations: Static review only; no tests, remote CI, protected credentials, real Gmail/private-repository behavior, production health, or final AC-033 were executed or inferred.
Existing sibling review records were intentionally not inspected.
summary: FAIL: the Stage 6 capacity/KILL-007 gate has an untested PRODUCTION_RUN bypass, so the assurance evidence cannot support its fail-closed operational claim.
